title: botrychium spathulatum
intro: Botrychium spathulatum, also known as the fan-leaved grape fern, is a type of rare and endangered vascular plant belonging to the family Osmundaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as aiding in wound healing and improving respiratory function. Herbal preparations of Botrychium spathulatum can be found in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to fully confirm its efficacy and safety.
